---
title: "Wrangling project"
author: "Sandi Alshahwan"
date: "2026-04-09"
output: word_document
---

```{r}
getwd()
```
```{r}
cumulative_data <- read.csv("combined_hesitancy_sdoh_cumulative_death_rate.txt")

dim(cumulative_data)
names(cumulative_data)
head(cumulative_data[, 1:15])
```

## Identify the last cumulative death column
```{r}
last_col_name <- names(cumulative_data)[ncol(cumulative_data)]
last_col_name

cumulative_data$death_outcome <- cumulative_data[[last_col_name]]

summary(cumulative_data$death_outcome)
```

## Check Key Variables
```{r}
names(cumulative_data)
```

## Hesitancy Vs Cumulative death
```{r}
cor.test(cumulative_data$Hesitancy_Rate, cumulative_data$death_outcome)
```

##SDOH Vs Cumulative death
```{r}
names(cumulative_data)
```
## Correlations
```{r}
## SDOH vs cumulative death correlations

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_Aged.65.years.or.older, cumulative_data$death_outcome)

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_Crowding, cumulative_data$death_outcome)

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_Housing.cost.burden, cumulative_data$death_outcome)

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_No.broadband, cumulative_data$death_outcome)

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_No.high.school.diploma, cumulative_data$death_outcome)

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_Poverty, cumulative_data$death_outcome)

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_Racial.or.ethnic.minority.status, cumulative_data$death_outcome)

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_Single.parent.households, cumulative_data$death_outcome)

cor.test(cumulative_data$SDOH_Unemployment, cumulative_data$death_outcome)
```
```{r}
library(officer)
library(flextable)
library(dplyr)

extract_cor_results <- function(data, var1, var2, label) {
  temp <- na.omit(data[, c(var1, var2)])
  test <- cor.test(temp[[var1]], temp[[var2]])
  
  r_value <- unname(test$estimate)
  ci_low <- test$conf.int[1]
  ci_high <- test$conf.int[2]
  p_val <- test$p.value
  
  interpretation <- case_when(
    abs(r_value) < 0.10 ~ "Very weak",
    abs(r_value) < 0.30 ~ "Weak",
    abs(r_value) < 0.50 ~ "Moderate",
    TRUE ~ "Strong"
  )
  
  direction <- ifelse(r_value >= 0, "positive", "negative")
  p_val_text <- ifelse(p_val < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", p_val))
  
  data.frame(
    Variable = label,
    Correlation = round(r_value, 2),
    CI_95 = paste0(round(ci_low, 2), " to ", round(ci_high, 2)),
    P_value = as.character(p_val_text),
    Interpretation = paste(interpretation, direction),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

# Table 1: Hesitancy vs cumulative death
table1 <- extract_cor_results(
  data = cumulative_data,
  var1 = "Hesitancy_Rate",
  var2 = "death_outcome",
  label = "Hesitancy Rate"
)

# Table 2: SDOH vs cumulative death
sdoh_vars <- c(
  "SDOH_Aged.65.years.or.older",
  "SDOH_Crowding",
  "SDOH_Housing.cost.burden",
  "SDOH_No.broadband",
  "SDOH_No.high.school.diploma",
  "SDOH_Poverty",
  "SDOH_Racial.or.ethnic.minority.status",
  "SDOH_Single.parent.households",
  "SDOH_Unemployment"
)

sdoh_labels <- c(
  "Aged 65+",
  "Crowding",
  "Housing Cost Burden",
  "No Broadband",
  "No High School Diploma",
  "Poverty",
  "Minority Status",
  "Single-Parent Households",
  "Unemployment"
)

table2 <- bind_rows(
  lapply(seq_along(sdoh_vars), function(i) {
    extract_cor_results(
      data = cumulative_data,
      var1 = sdoh_vars[i],
      var2 = "death_outcome",
      label = sdoh_labels[i]
    )
  })
)

# Make tables
ft1 <- flextable(table1)
ft1 <- autofit(ft1)

ft2 <- flextable(table2)
ft2 <- autofit(ft2)

# Export to Word
doc <- read_docx()

doc <- body_add_par(doc, "Correlation Results: Cumulative Death Analysis", style = "heading 1")
doc <- body_add_par(doc, "Table 1. Hesitancy Rate vs Cumulative Death Rate", style = "heading 2")
doc <- body_add_flextable(doc, ft1)

doc <- body_add_par(doc, "", style = "Normal")

doc <- body_add_par(doc, "Table 2. Social Determinants of Health vs Cumulative Death Rate", style = "heading 2")
doc <- body_add_flextable(doc, ft2)

print(doc, target = "cumulative_death_correlation_tables.docx")
```

## Two predictor regression (hesitancy + SDOH = Cumulative death)
```{r}
library(officer)
library(flextable)
library(dplyr)

# Function to extract model results for Hesitancy + one SDOH predictor
extract_lm_results <- function(data, sdoh_var, sdoh_label, outcome = "death_outcome") {
  
  # Keep only needed columns and drop missing
  temp <- data[, c("Hesitancy_Rate", sdoh_var, outcome)]
  temp <- na.omit(temp)
  
  # Fit model
  model <- lm(
    as.formula(paste(outcome, "~ Hesitancy_Rate +", sdoh_var)),
    data = temp
  )
  
  model_summary <- summary(model)
  coefs <- model_summary$coefficients
  
  # Pull hesitancy row
  hes_est <- coefs["Hesitancy_Rate", "Estimate"]
  hes_p   <- coefs["Hesitancy_Rate", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  
  # Pull SDOH row
  sdoh_est <- coefs[sdoh_var, "Estimate"]
  sdoh_p   <- coefs[sdoh_var, "Pr(>|t|)"]
  
  # Model fit
  r2 <- model_summary$r.squared
  adj_r2 <- model_summary$adj.r.squared
  
  data.frame(
    SDOH_Variable = sdoh_label,
    Hesitancy_Beta = round(hes_est, 2),
    Hesitancy_P = ifelse(hes_p < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", hes_p)),
    SDOH_Beta = round(sdoh_est, 2),
    SDOH_P = ifelse(sdoh_p < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", sdoh_p)),
    R_squared = round(r2, 3),
    Adjusted_R_squared = round(adj_r2, 3),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

# Run all 9 two-predictor models
sdoh_vars <- c(
  "SDOH_Aged.65.years.or.older",
  "SDOH_Crowding",
  "SDOH_Housing.cost.burden",
  "SDOH_No.broadband",
  "SDOH_No.high.school.diploma",
  "SDOH_Poverty",
  "SDOH_Racial.or.ethnic.minority.status",
  "SDOH_Single.parent.households",
  "SDOH_Unemployment"
)

sdoh_labels <- c(
  "Aged 65+",
  "Crowding",
  "Housing Cost Burden",
  "No Broadband",
  "No High School Diploma",
  "Poverty",
  "Minority Status",
  "Single-Parent Households",
  "Unemployment"
)

table3 <- bind_rows(
  lapply(seq_along(sdoh_vars), function(i) {
    extract_lm_results(
      data = cumulative_data,
      sdoh_var = sdoh_vars[i],
      sdoh_label = sdoh_labels[i],
      outcome = "death_outcome"
    )
  })
)

# View table in R
table3

# Make Word table
ft3 <- flextable(table3)
ft3 <- autofit(ft3)

# Export Table 3 to Word
doc3 <- read_docx()

doc3 <- body_add_par(doc3, "Table 3. Two-Predictor Models for Cumulative Death Rate", style = "heading 1")
doc3 <- body_add_par(
  doc3,
  "Each model includes Hesitancy Rate and one SDOH variable as correlates of cumulative death rate.",
  style = "Normal"
)
doc3 <- body_add_flextable(doc3, ft3)

print(doc3, target = "table3_two_predictor_models_cumulative.docx")
```
